label: 143 Wright Brothers Day

Centennial of Flight Commemoration Act, , , as amended by , , , known as the Centennial of Flight Commemoration Act, established the Centennial of Flight Commission to carry out certain activities related to the history of aviation and the commemoration of the centennial of powered flight, required the Commission to make annual reports and a final report not later than , and provided that the Commission terminate not later than 60 days after submission of the final report.Pub. children.heading: .—Purpose
children.label: b .—Purpose
children.text: Wright Brothers Day commemorates the first successful flights in a heavier than air, mechanically propelled airplane, that were made by Orville and Wilbur Wright on , near Kitty Hawk, North Carolina.December 17, 1903

children.heading: .—Proclamation
children.label: c .—Proclamation
children.text: The President is requested to issue each year a proclamation inviting the people of the United States to observe Wright Brothers Day with appropriate ceremonies and activities.
